rules: [P1] When asked for a patient identifier (e.g., MRN) given name and DOB, query the patient directory and finish with FINISH([identifier]).
[P1] Parse complex vital‑sign strings (e.g., blood pressure) into components for decision logic.
[P1] Use FINISH([...]) only for JSON array responses (e.g., order IDs); output raw values or plain text otherwise, and never include narrative text outside FINISH.
[P1] Do not fabricate, guess, or use placeholder data; use only retrieved information and omit explanatory or reasoning text.
[P1] Verify patient existence via GET using MRN (or name/DOB) before any operation; if not found, output the exact phrase Patient not found without any FINISH or JSON wrapper.
[P1] Compare measurements against explicit clinical thresholds; if within normal range, return FINISH([]) and do not create orders.
[P1] When creating referrals or posting new observations, embed any required free‑text verbatim in the POST request and finish with FINISH([]) only.
[P1] Only create conditional orders (medication and/or labs) when the primary clinical trigger is satisfied.
[P1] First GET the patient by identifier, then GET the observation, evaluate its timestamp, and conditionally POST a lab order before FINISH.
[P1] Include appropriate query filters (e.g., test name, time range) in GET requests.
[P1] Never embed reasoning or instructions inside the action payload.
[P1] Output exactly one action per turn using the defined JSON schema; no explanatory text.
[P1] When multiple API calls are required, chain them across successive turns.
